source.title: Fire Incident during Construction Operations at HI A 379 B Platform

context.region: Gulf of Mexico, HI Area Block A 379

actions_taken: Fire extinguished using one 125-pound ABC wheeled fire extinguisher, two 30-pound fire extinguishers, and three firewater hose reels
M/V Melissa directed to return to platform and placed on standby
BSEE directed platform to remain offline until cause determined and hazards mitigated
event.summary: On February 21, 2024, at approximately 1349 hours, a fire occurred at the HI A 379 B platform operated by W&T Offshore during construction operations to replace corroded deck plating. While a construction fitter was grinding freshly laid welds, sparks migrated through two layers of protective fire blanket barriers into a chemical/methanol storage area approximately six feet away, igniting flammable materials. The fire was extinguished using multiple fire extinguishers and hose reels. The facility was shut down via ESD. Investigation revealed that flammable materials should have been removed per JSA requirements rather than relying on fire blanket barriers.
recommendations: All approved welding and burning plans should state the proper Code of Federal Regulations must be followed
Ensure flammable or combustible items are removed from hot work areas as per JSA requirements
Improve pre-job safety meetings to ensure all written procedures are discussed and followed
Resolve contradictions between approved General Welding and Burning Plan and Hot Work Checklist
key_phrases: sparks migrated through two layers of protective fire blanket barriers
Remove all FLAMMABLE OR COMBUSTABLE ITEMS
no discussion or consideration was given to the two written requirements
swirling winds generated during a helicopter landing operation

threats.name: Sparks from grinding operations migrating to flammable materials
Inadequate separation between hot work and flammable storage
Helicopter downwash carrying sparks to flammable materials
Failure to remove flammable materials from hot work area

consequences.name: Equipment damage (melted poly tanks, electrical cable)

controls.name: Fire blanket barriers between work area and chemical storage
controls.barrier_role: Physical barrier to prevent spark migration
controls.lod_basis: Primary physical control to prevent ignition

supporting_text: sparks migrated through two (2) layers of protective fire blanket barriers into a chemical/methanol storage area
The construction crew elected to use the third control measure of the W&T Offshore Hot Work Checklist, which was erecting fire blanket barriers between the work area and the chemical storage area
Utilizing the mentioned control measure did not eliminate the hazard

controls.name: Removal of flammable materials from hot work area (per JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Primary administrative control to eliminate hazard

supporting_text: The Zealous Job Safety Analysis (JSA) clearly states 'Remove all FLAMMABLE OR COMBUSTABLE ITEMS, Make sure no Hydrocarbons are present.'
Storage and chemical tote tanks could have been moved to another area
Written procedures in submitted documents clearly states to remove hydrocarbons prior to hot work commencing
no discussion or consideration was given to the two written requirements cited in the Zealous JSA

line-item number 17 of the W&T Offshore Hot Work Checklist states, 'Equipment containing hydrocarbons or other flammable substances is relocated at least 35 feet horizontally from the welding site... or is otherwise protected with flame-proof covers...' This line item was checked as yes.
The construction crew elected to use the third control measure of the W&T Offshore Hot Work Checklist
While reviewing W&T approved General Welding and Burning Plan this document contradicts the W&T Hot Work Checklist

controls.name: Pre-job safety meeting
controls.barrier_role: Communication and verification of safety requirements

supporting_text: Interviews with both the Zealous Foreman and the W&T PIC found that no discussion or consideration was given to the two written requirements cited in the Zealous JSA and the W&T Hot Work Safety Checklist
it is the responsibility of the PIC and Task Supervisor (Construction Foreman) to ensure control measures or in place and effective
the Firewatch notified the construction crew, and all work was stopped

controls.name: Emergency Shut Down (ESD) system
controls.barrier_role: Isolation of hydrocarbon sources during emergency

supporting_text: The alarm was then sounded and an Emergency Shut Down (ESD) was initiated, which shut-in the facility
The fire was extinguished using one (1) 125-pound (#) ABC wheeled fire extinguisher, two (2) 30 # fire extinguishers, and three (3) firewater hoses reels
Equipment containing hydrocarbons or other flammable substances is relocated at least 35 feet horizontally from the welding site
30 CFR 250.113(a) which states in part 'Before you weld, you must move any equipment containing hydrocarbons or other flammable substances at least 35 feet horizontally from the welding area'
chemical/methanol storage area located just behind the protective barrier, approximately six feet (6') from the grinding area

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
